command: dism /Mount-Image /ImageFile:C:\images\install_clean.wim /Index:1 /MountDir:C:\Mount\Base; dism /Mount-Image /ImageFile:C:\images\install_mod.wim /Index:1 /MountDir:C:\Mount\Win; New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path C:\Mount\Win\Windows\Setup\Scripts | Out-Null; Set-Content -Path C:\Mount\Win\Windows\Setup\Scripts\SetupComplete.cmd -Value "rem placeholder"; Compare-Object (Get-ChildItem -Name C:\Mount\Base\Windows\Setup\Scripts -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) (Get-ChildItem -Name C:\Mount\Win\Windows\Setup\Scripts -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)
explanation: Mounts clean and modified Windows images, drops a SetupComplete.cmd into the modified image (offline persistence), and uses Compare-Object to verify the added script versus the clean baseline.
rationale_steps: Mount base and target WIMs with DISM (Admin-only operation).
Modify the offline image by adding SetupComplete.cmd.
List files in both images' Scripts directories and Compare-Object to detect the new file.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: Well-formed PowerShell one-liner with valid DISM flags, balanced quotes/parentheses, and correct separators.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: It mounts clean and modified WIMs and adds SetupComplete.cmd to the mounted image aligning with image tampering, but omits committing/unmounting the image so changes may not persist and it requires admin despite 'Privilege: None'.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: All commands are well-formed with balanced quotes, valid DISM flags, proper PowerShell cmdlet syntax, and correct semicolon separators throughout.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command correctly mounts clean and modified WIM images, injects a SetupComplete.cmd persistence script into the offline image (a realistic T1601 technique for modifying a Windows image), and uses Compare-Object to diff the two mounts for verification; the minor gap is that 'None' privilege is unrealistic since DISM image mounting typically requires administrator rights.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command uses well-formed DISM syntax and standard PowerShell cmdlets with correct separators, flags, and balanced parentheses.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command accurately implements T1601 by using DISM to mount a Windows image and injecting a SetupComplete.cmd file for offline persistence, followed by a verification step.
